item.title: Seed Stress Test - Mathematics Knowledge Base
item.description: Seed Stress Test: Mathematics Knowledge Base Testing Seed rules against the demands of mathematical knowledge capture.

item.title: AI-Assisted Knowledge Management Seed
item.description: AI-Assisted Knowledge Management Seed Drop this file into any vault to bootstrap a healthy, self-improving knowledge base.

item.title: Frontier Exploration - Knowledge Applicability Boundaries
item.description: Frontier Exploration - Knowledge Applicability Boundaries Capturing when knowledge doesn’t apply — the inverse of scope tags The Gap The Seed covers scope tags (jurisdiction, hardiness-zone, expertise-level) and trade-off knowledge.

item.title: Frontier Exploration - Vault Query Patterns for Decision-Making
item.description: Vault Query Patterns for Decision-Making The Seed covers building and maintaining knowledge vaults but lacks guidance on querying them for decisions.

item.title: Frontier Gap - Skill Proficiency Tracking
item.description: Frontier Gap: Skill Proficiency Tracking in Knowledge Bases Knowledge of “how to do X” differs fundamentally from the ability to actually do X.

item.title: Note Types and Templates
item.description: Note Types and Templates Different notes serve different functions in a knowledge base. This note covers both the types and standardized formats.

item.title: Seed Improvement - Safety-Critical Knowledge Metadata
item.description: Seed Improvement - Safety-Critical Knowledge Metadata Adding safety metadata to Seed for domains where missing safety information could cause harm Problem The Seed doesn’t distinguish safety-critical knowledge from regular knowledge.

item.title: The Inverse Test
item.description: The Inverse Test A technique for discovering undocumented knowledge boundaries — companion to Frontier Exploration - Knowledge Applicability Boundaries The Method For any absolute statement (always/never/every/none), ask inverse questions: “Always? Under what conditions?” “Never? In what contexts?” ...

item.title: Types of Applicability Boundaries
item.description: Types of Applicability Boundaries Categorization of how knowledge boundaries can be documented — companion to Frontier Exploration - Knowledge Applicability Boundaries Knowledge doesn’t apply in different ways.

item.title: Frontier Exploration - Building Knowledge Bases in Unfamiliar Domains
item.description: Frontier Exploration: Building Knowledge Bases in Unfamiliar Domains What to do when the AI doesn’t understand the topic it’s helping to document The Problem The Seed assumes an AI agent can: Understand the domain it’s working in Identify what constitutes quality knowledge Recognize when knowledge i...
